actions.name: create_a_collection
actions.description: Tool to create a new Postman collection in a specific workspace or the default workspace. Use when you need to create a collection with workspace specification. For complete collection format details, refer to the Postman Collection Format documentation.

workspace.description: Optional workspace ID where the collection will be created. If not specified, the collection is created in the user's default workspace

name.description: The name of the collection to create

schema.description: The schema version URL for the collection. Defaults to v2.1.0

description.description: Optional description of the collection explaining its purpose

info.description: Collection metadata containing name, description, and schema version

item.description: Nested items if this is a folder

name.description: Name of the item (request or folder)

request.description: Request object containing method, url, headers, body, etc.

items.description: Represents a collection item which can be a request or folder.

item.description: Array of collection items (requests, folders). Defaults to empty array for new collections

collection.description: Collection object containing info (name, description, schema) and item array (requests/folders)

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for creating a new Postman collection.

id.description: Unique identifier of the created collection

collection.description: Created collection object containing id, name, and uid

actions.name: create_a_collection_comment
actions.description: Tool to create a comment on an API's collection. Use when you need to add a comment to a specific collection within an API. To create a reply on an existing comment, include the thread_id in the request.

body.description: The text content of the comment to create on the API collection.

api_id.description: The API's unique identifier (UUID). This identifies the API containing the collection.

thread_id.description: Optional thread ID to reply to an existing comment thread. Include this to create a reply on an existing comment.

collection_id.description: The collection's unique identifier (UUID). This identifies the specific collection to comment on.

id.description: The comment's unique ID

body.description: The text content of the comment

thread_id.description: The thread ID this comment belongs to

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the comment was created

created_by.description: The ID of the user who created the comment

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the comment was last updated

actions.name: create_a_collection_from_a_schema
actions.description: Tool to create a collection from a schema and link it to an API with specified relations. Note: This endpoint is deprecated in Postman v10 and higher. Use when you need to generate a collection from an API schema and establish relations like contract tests or documentation.

api_id.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the API

id.description: The unique identifier of the created relation
The unique identifier of the created collection

uid.description: The extended unique identifier combining workspace and collection ID

actions.name: create_a_mock_server
actions.description: Tool to create a new mock server in a Postman collection. Use when you need to create a mock server to simulate API endpoints for testing or development. Returns the created mock server's details including the mockUrl which can be used to make requests.

actions.name: create_a_monitor
actions.description: Tool to create a new monitor in a specific workspace to run a collection on a schedule. Use when you need to set up automated collection runs at specified intervals using cron expressions within a workspace.

monitor.description: Monitor configuration object containing name, collection, schedule, and optional environment

workspace.description: The workspace ID where the monitor will be created

id.description: Unique identifier of the created monitor

monitor.description: The created monitor object with id, name, and uid

actions.name: create_a_pull_request

collection_uid.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the forked collection from which to create the pull request. Format: {owner}-{collectionId}

actions.name: create_a_schema
actions.description: Tool to create a schema for an API in Postman. Use when you need to add a schema definition (such as OpenAPI, GraphQL, or Protocol Buffers) to an existing API. The schema can consist of single or multiple files. Returns the created schema's ID and metadata upon successful creation.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for replacing an environment's data.

id.description: Unique identifier of the environment

actions.name: replace_collections_data_asynchronously
actions.description: Tool to replace the entire contents of a collection asynchronously. Use when you need to completely replace a collection with new data. IMPORTANT: Include the collection's ID values in item, variable, and other nested objects to preserve them. If you do not include IDs, existing items will be removed and new items will be created.

schema.description: The schema version URL for the collection. Defaults to v2.1.0

description.description: Optional description of the collection explaining its purpose

info.description: Collection metadata containing name, description, and schema version

id.description: The unique identifier for the item. Include to preserve existing items, omit to create new ones.

anyOf.description: The list of item item option 2 values.

item.description: Nested items if this is a folder

name.description: Name of the item (request or folder)

request.description: Request object containing method, url, headers, body, etc.

anyOf.description: The list of request item option 2 values.
The list of response item option 2 values.

response.description: Array of saved responses for this request

id.description: The unique identifier for the variable. Include to preserve existing variables, omit to create new ones.

variable.description: Collection-level variables. Include existing variable IDs to preserve them, omit IDs to create new variables.

collection.description: The complete collection object to replace the existing collection. IMPORTANT: Include the collection's ID values in item, variable, and other nested objects to preserve them. If you do not include IDs, the endpoint removes existing items and creates new items.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for replacing collection contents asynchronously.

id.description: The unique identifier of the collection

schema.description: The schema URL for the collection format

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the collection was last updated

description.description: A description of the collection

collection.description: The replaced collection object. The update happens asynchronously, so full details may not be immediately available.

actions.name: sync_collection_with_schema
actions.description: Tool to sync a collection attached to an API with the API schema. This is an asynchronous endpoint that returns HTTP 202 Accepted. Use when you need to synchronize a collection with changes made to the API schema. The collection must already be attached to the API. Returns a task ID that can be used to check the status of the sync operation.

api_id.description: The unique identifier of the API. Must be in UUID format.

collection_uid.description: The unique identifier of the collection that is attached to the API. Format: userId-collectionId.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters to sync a collection with an API schema.

taskId.description: The unique identifier of the asynchronous sync task. Use this to check task status.

location.description: URL to check the status of the sync task

actions.name: sync_collection_with_spec
actions.description: Tool to sync a collection generated from an API specification. This is an asynchronous operation that returns HTTP 202 Accepted. Use when you need to update a collection to match the latest version of its source API specification. The collection must have been generated from a spec.

spec_id.description: The ID of the API specification to sync with the collection. This is the source specification that the collection was generated from.

collection_uid.description: The UID of a collection that was generated from an API specification. This collection will be synchronized with its source specification.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters to sync a collection with its API specification.

url.description: The URL to poll for checking the status of the synchronization task

taskId.description: The unique identifier of the asynchronous task for tracking the synchronization operation

actions.name: sync_spec_with_collection
actions.description: Tool to sync an API specification with a linked collection. This is an asynchronous operation that returns HTTP 202 Accepted with task tracking information. Use when you need to synchronize changes from a generated collection back to its source specification. Prerequisites: the collection must be generated from the spec, and the spec must be single-file.

spec_id.description: The unique identifier of the API specification to sync. Must be in UUID format.

collection_uid.description: The unique identifier of the generated collection whose changes should be synchronized back to the specification. The collection must have been generated from this spec, and the spec must be a single-file specification.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for syncing an API specification with a collection.

url.description: The relative URL to check the task status. Use GET request to this URL to monitor sync progress.

taskId.description: The unique identifier for the asynchronous sync task. Use this to poll the task status.

actions.name: transfer_folders
actions.description: Tool to copy or move folders into a collection or folder. Use when you need to reorganize collections by transferring folders between collections or into other folders.

ids.description: Array of folder unique identifiers (UIDs) to transfer. Each UID must be in format 'owner-folderId' (e.g., '50304422-f172eb50-ded9-dcc1-5c6a-1ad2a604bf55'). All folders must exist and be accessible.

mode.description: Transfer mode: 'copy' creates duplicates of folders in target, 'move' relocates folders to target (removes from source).

id.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the target collection or folder in format 'owner-id' (e.g., '50304422-747b3035-ab7d-408e-bff9-ac1634a769d4')

model.description: Type of target to transfer folders into. Use 'collection' to transfer into a collection root, or 'folder' to transfer into a specific folder.

target.description: Target location where folders should be transferred. Specify the model type (collection or folder) and its UID.

id.description: Optional ID to position relative to. Use with model to place folders before/after a specific item.

model.description: Optional model type for positioning relative to another item. Can be 'collection', 'folder', or 'request'.

position.description: Position where folders should be placed in the target. Use 'start' to place at beginning or 'end' to place at end.

location.description: Position within the target where folders should be placed. Specify 'start' or 'end' for the position.

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for transferring folders between collections or into folders.

ids.description: Array of folder IDs created in the target location after the transfer operation. These are the new folder UIDs in the destination.

actions.name: update_part_of_a_collection
actions.description: Tool to update specific collection properties like name, description, authentication, variables, or events. Use when you need to partially update a collection without replacing the entire collection structure. Returns the updated collection information after the changes are applied.

name.description: The new name for the collection

description.description: The new description for the collection

exec.description: Array of script lines to execute

items.description: Event script to update in the collection.

event.description: Collection-level event scripts to update or add

items.description: Variable to update or add at the collection level.

variable.description: Collection-level variables to update or add

collection.description: The collection object containing the properties to update. Can include info (with name, description), auth, variables, or events properties. Only the specified properties will be updated.

collection_id.description: The unique identifier (ID or UID) of the collection to update

inputSchema.description: Request parameters for updating specific collection properties.

schema.description: The schema URL for the collection format

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the collection was last updated

description.description: A description of the collection

exec.description: Array of script lines to execute

id.description: The unique identifier for the variable

collection.description: The updated collection object with the modified properties
